title: HIC Kitchen Mortar and Pestle for Grinding Spices and Herbs and Crushing Pills, Fine-White Porcelain
description:
About this item
HIC Kitchen’s Mortar and Pestle Set for grinding items into a fine paste or powder
Made from fine-white porcelain; complements any decor; mortar 2.75 x 1.5-inches; pestle 2.875-inches
Quickly grind and break down ingredients; speeds up food preparation with minimum effort
Make aromatic dry rubs, curry pastes, pestos, nut butters, and ground cinnamon, or grind peppercorns, prepare holistic remedies, crush pills, and more
Easy to use and clean; dishwasher safe for easy cleanup
reviews_5s:
This is a standard small mortar and pestle set. They work well and don't break the bank so they get a good review in my book. They are small but if you are just throwing together some spices for cooking or some incense, its actually a nice little size. I like that it fits in my palm comfortably and I can really grind with them. If you need a large set this is not the one. If you are looking to grind really hard items, this probably won't work. But if you are just using it for the normal small stuff, this is perfect..I wanted this to grind a specific spice in. Perfect fit. Only thing, I didn't realize it came from the type of company it came from. I will be more careful from now on. I'm keeping it, but before I use it, I will have it blessed by my priest. As for the product itself, it is strong, heavy for it's size, durable, & well made. It's much smaller than I thought, but it's cute! Perfect for crushing just a few spices.Despite being told the measurements I am chronically awful at figuring out how small or large something is without seeing it. A tiny little mortar and pestle showed up and can fit comfortably in my tiny lady sized palm (apparently I have "embroidery hands"). At first I balked thinking it was too small and I'd bought the wrong size, but no! This tiny thing is amazing at its' job. I've been crushing spices and herbs for various uses (air freshening, carpet freshening, cooking, not all at the same time!) and it chews through them all with no problems.

Obviously, it's a heavy stick and a bowl, so the crushing process is some real work but the work it does is exactly what I wanted. Larger herbs and spices will have to be broken down a bit to fit inside but I comfortably got a whole smallish cinnamon stick in it with just a few breaks and had rough cinnamon powder in a few minutes.

It does have a minor staining problem because the inside isn't glazed the same way but it comes clean every time with ease. I love it! I'll gladly buy another but I'm pretty sure this thing is going to survive like a tank.I love this. It is the perfect small size for grinding my specialty course saltGood productUsed for grinding seeds to cook with while traveling. Love itPlay Video
For such a tiny little thing, it does the job really well. I intentionally bought it because the plastic pill crusher just broke within days of using it. This has been a life saver and enjoyable to useAbsolutely perfect for small projects. I use this to crush up vitamins for my daughter and put them into tiny capsules that she can swallow. This is great for that. So many awesome things to love!

Pros:

1) effective. It works beautifully! The mortar and pestle are a perfect pair, and when you gently smash into the side nothing splashes out. I love that there is very little cleanup to this set.

2) compact and easy to store. Who isn't incredibly tired of having too much big stuff? I'm over it! This helps because it is about the size of the palm of your hand and yet it is deep enough to have some use. Perfect size!

3) dishwasher safe - 'nuff said!

Cons:

Absolutely no cons. Love this thing! It gets used almost daily here. I waited forever because I didn't want a big thing to store. No more wait - this is perfect!This product is exactly what I was looking for, didn't need to spend alot of money. Great value for your dollar.Found this on sale. It arrived quicker than expected. The size is perfect for my use - grinding pills to put in gel capsules for my pet . The small size makes it easy to use and clean.
reviews_1s:
I've owned this for one month and am now buying a proper STONE mortar and pestle to replace this. I bought it to crush my daily thyroid pill and it seemed the perfect size for it. I thought I really liked it until the other day I noticed a shard of ceramic actually came off from the inside of the bowl. It was tiny and sharp and I counted my lucky stars that I didn't crush it with my pill or accidentally swallow that sharp piece whole. It made me worry that perhaps I've crushed other tiny pieces of the ceramic in the past and swallowed them, as the texture of the inside of the bowl has changed over the last month in addition to the spot where it chipped. Since then I've found out that a proper mortar and pestle is supposed to be made of stone so that this doesn't happen. I do not recommend this item as I believe it is a potential hazard. Please research and buy a proper stone mortar and pestle and keep yourself safe.Too small to really work with. Chipped it straight away. Definitely wouldn’t buy again.I was truly disappointed. I should have paid closer attention to the reviews because the actual product you receive varies greatly. Mine seems sturdy enough. The pestle is shaped properly, but the mortar isn't, so they don't make full contact, which makes it extremely difficult to actually crush or mix anything.

Unlike sets I've used in the past, the bottom part of the pestle and the inside of the mortar don't have a ceramic coating. Dust really sticks to this. Also, that texture might work for DRY spices, but avoid anything with moisture.

I got this for mixing my own watercolor paints. The texture of this makes all the paint stick and soak in, making it impossible to remove and put into a paint pan, so all my paint was wasted down the drain.Left this review once already, but Amazon says it violates community standards. The listing says this thing is 4 inches wide, but it is only 2 1/2 inches wide. Plain and simple, not as described. If that violates community standards, maybe I should shop somewhere else.We used the pestle and mortar to powder a medication for a fussy dog. The pill is not that hard, but the bowl chipped almost immediately, developed a crack, and today, broke altogether, just two weeks from when it was ordered. Not acceptable.It does the job if I was crushing pills. Too small for cooking in the kitchen ie ginger, garlic and such. Would not rebuy.The first mortar and pestle I ordered arrived DOA: the mortar was in three pieces. I returned it to Amazon who replaced it with one that was cracked and even the glaze didn't cover the crack. Didn't return the second one. I have better things to do with my time than paddleboard-ball back and forth to the UPS store. I took some pix of the second mortar, but there isn't a way to include any here, and this POS isn't worth the effort of a video review. The people at HIC apparently have little or no quality control/assurance, nor does their shipping department.
I've since found a nice little mortar and pestle at a local kitchen store where I also met a very cute and knowledgable sales lady. Silver linings can occur in the most unexpected places.The mortar and pestle appears at first to be a good one but as I felt the "porcelain" like finish inside the bowl the smooth finish on the outside and upper inside of the bowl ended and in the area where the pestle is to grind the things I will be using it for the finish is rough, unglazed and easily stained. It is nothing but something to look at.It's not big enough to grand but 1 pill . Sure isn't worth the price.made a bad choice with it
